questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given underlined word. Climbing without proper gear can be hazardous to your life.
questions.explanation: A) Hazardous (adjective) – Dangerous, risky, unsafe, toxic, harmful. खतरनाकAntonym: Safe (adjective) – Free from harm, danger, or risk. सुरक्षित•Toxic (adjective)– Poisonous, harmful, lethal. विषैला•Risky (adjective)– Involving danger, uncertain, hazardous. जोखिमपूर्ण•Dangerous (adjective)– Likely to cause harm or injury. खतरनाक

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words in the given sentence The money spent by the government on the development of machinery, equipment, building, health facilities, education, etc.
questions.explanation: B)Capex(noun) – The money spent by the government on the development of machinery, equipment, building, health facilities, education, etc. पूंजीगत व्यय Debacle(noun) – a sudden and complete failure. पतन Antecedent(noun) – a thing or event that existed before or logically precedes another. पूर्वज Concord(noun) – agreement or harmony between people or groups. सामंजस्य

questions.question: Select the correctly spelt word to fill in the blank and make the sentence meaningful. Before retiring, Kaul had been teaching physics at the university for three_________________.
questions.explanation: B) यहाँ'decades'का use सही होगा क्योंकि 'decades' का अर्थ है दशकों। sentence में बताया गया है कि कौल ने विश्वविद्यालय में भौतिकी पढ़ाई थी और 'तीन दशकों' का संदर्भ दिया गया है। 'Decimals' का अर्थ है दशमलव, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Decadents' का अर्थ है नैतिक या सांस्कृतिक पतन का व्यक्ति, जो वाक्य के अर्थ के अनुसार उपयुक्त नहीं है। 'Edecamals' का कोई मान्य अर्थ नहीं है और यह गलत spelling है। The correct word is 'decades' because it means a period of ten years. The sentence talks about Kaul teaching physics at the university for "three decades," making this option contextually accurate. 'Decimals' refers to a numerical system based on the number ten, which doesn't fit the context here. 'Decadents' means morally or culturally declining individuals, which isn't relevant to the sentence. 'Edecamals' is not a valid word and is incorrectly spelled.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given italicize word. She chose the unglamorous path of social work over a high-paying corporate job.
questions.explanation: A)Unglamorous(adjective) – Not appealing or exciting; lacking glamour. साधारणAntonym:Attractive(adjective) – Pleasing or appealing to the senses. आकर्षक•Plain(adjective) – Simple, not ornate or elaborate. साधारण•Humble(adjective) – Modest, not showing off. विनम्र•Unrewarding(adjective) – Not providing satisfaction or profit. लाभहीन

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A) This is accompanied by due consideration to vulnerable sections that pay an inordinate price in most economic upheavals. All this has been accomplished with a spirit of fiscal rectitude. India deserves its moment in the limelight. B) Economic growth surprised on the upside in 2022-23, buoyed by momentum in the last quarter of the year. C) At 7.2%, India remains the fastest-growing major economy for the second year running, after clocking 9.1% in 2021-22. D) On current projections of 6.5% GDP growth in 2023-24, it could be headed for a hat-trick. With an average annual rate of 7.6% growth in three years since the pandemic, New Delhi has crafted one of the world's smartest economic recoveries while continuing to negotiate a series of unfolding global crises.
questions.explanation: C) BCDA

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the underlined spelling error. The permisible limit of vehicle speed has been increased in India by the Road Transport Corporation.
questions.explanation: A) The correct spelling of ‘permisible’is ‘permissible,’ which means "allowed or permitted by law or regulations." In Hindi, it means " आज्ञा पाने योग्य "।

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given italicize word. The finance minister announced new policies to ring-fence public sector banks from external economic shocks.
questions.explanation: A) Ring-fence (verb)– protect, insulate, isolate, shield, safeguard(सुरक्षित करना / अलग करना)Synonym:Protect(verb) – Guard or shield from harm(सुरक्षित करना)Expose(verb) – Make vulnerable or open(उजागर करना)Merge(verb) – Combine(मिलाना)Sell(verb) – Trade for money(बेचना)

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: A) The incorrectly spelt word is ‘Riside’, and the correct spelling is ‘Reside’. "किसी विशेष स्थान पर रहना।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. I am not going to pay a ________ for this broken CD.
questions.explanation: B)'Cent'का अर्थ है एक छोटी मुद्रा इकाई (currency unit) जो डॉलर का सौवां भाग होती है। यहाँ sentence में यह बताया गया है कि व्यक्ति एक खराब सीडी के लिए एक पैसा भी नहीं देगा। इस संदर्भ में 'cent' सही शब्द है। 'Scent' का अर्थ है सुगंध या गंध, जो इस context में अप्रासंगिक है। 'Send' का अर्थ है भेजना, जो यहाँ व्याकरणिक रूप से गलत है। 'Sent' 'send' का past form है, जिसका इस sentence में कोई संदर्भ नहीं है। 'Cent'refers to a small unit of currency, specifically one-hundredth of a dollar. In the sentence, it is mentioned that the person is not willing to pay even a single penny for a broken CD, making 'cent' the correct choice. 'Scent' means a smell or fragrance, which is irrelevant in this context. 'Send' means to dispatch, which is grammatically incorrect here. 'Sent' is the past tense of 'send' and does not fit the context of the sentence.

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given italicize word. He suffers from chronic back pain that has lasted for over a decade.
questions.explanation: C)Chronic(adjective) – persistent, long-term, constant, prolonged, habitual (दीर्घकालिक)Synonym:Persistent(adjective) – continuing for a long time (लगातार)Temporary(adjective) – lasting for a short time (अस्थायी)Instant(adjective) – immediate (तत्काल)Fleeting(adjective) – passing quickly (क्षणिक)

questions.question: Select the word to fill in the blank which means the same as the group of words in the given sentence _______________ is refers to a political, religious, or social system prevailing at a particular time.
questions.explanation: C)Dispensation(noun) – A political, religious, or social system prevailing at a particular time व्यवस्था Libel(noun) – a published false statement that is damaging to a person's reputation. मानहानि Parody(noun) – an imitation of the style of a particular writer, artist, or genre with deliberate exaggeration for comic effect. हास्य अनुकरण Cliché(noun) – an overused phrase or opinion that lacks original thought. घिसी-पिटी बात

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A building where animals are butchered.
questions.explanation: C)Abattoir(noun) – A building where animals are butchered. वधशाला Cemetery(noun): A large burial ground for burying dead people. कब्रिस्तान Decanter(noun): A glass container used to serve wine or other drinks. शराब की सुराही Burrow(noun): A hole or tunnel dug by a small animal for habitation. बिल

questions.question: Select the best express Synonym of the given italicize word. The village school provided only a rudimentary education due to a lack of resources.
questions.explanation: C)Rudimentary(adjective) – basic, elementary, undeveloped, crude, primitive प्रारंभिक / अधूराSynonym:Basic(adjective) – essential or elementary (मूलभूत)Advanced(adjective) – highly developed (विकसित)Developed(adjective) – grown or evolved (पूर्णतः विकसित)Profound(adjective) – deep or intense (गहरा)

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank to complete the idiom and make the sentence meaningful. Her company's new product launch was a real _____________ squib.
questions.explanation: A) Damp squib (idiom) - a situation or event which is much less impressive than expected. एक घटना या स्थिति जो उम्मीद से कम प्रभावी या निराशाजनक हो।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank and complete the given idiom. That’s great! You keep everything under your _________________.
questions.explanation: B)Under someone’s thumb(idiom) – To be under someone's control or influence. किसी के नियंत्रण या प्रभाव में होना।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate expression that can substitute the underlined words in the given sentence. The watchman tried every possible way to catch the thief of the hostel.
questions.explanation: B)Explored every avenue(idiom) – tried every possible method or way to achieve something सभी विकल्पों को आज़माना Cut the cackle(idiom) – to stop talking and focus on the important matter at hand. बातचीत बंद करना और मुख्य मुद्दे पर ध्यान केंद्रित करना। Arrived at the eleventh hour(idiom) – to arrive or act just in time or at the last possible moment. आखिरी समय में पहुंचना। Called it a day(idiom) – to stop working or to end an activity for the day. काम या गतिविधि को समाप्त करना।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Pay off old scores
questions.explanation: A)Pay off old scores(idiom) – To have one's revenge (बदला लेना)

questions.question: Select the sentence that has a grammatical error
options: Suhas has a younger brother and an elder sister.
It was a huge zoo with an elephant enclosure.
Alka bought new pair of shoes for the party.
He was an honest engineer by profession.
questions.explanation: C)Alka bought new pair of shoes for the party इस वाक्य में "new pair of shoes" से पहले आर्टिकल 'a' का use नहीं किया गया है। Rule: जब भी किसी singular countable noun का उल्लेख किया जाता है, तो उसके पहले 'a' या 'an' जैसे article का use किया जाना चाहिए। सही वाक्य होगा: "Alka bought a new pair of shoes for the party. In this sentence, the article 'a' is missing before "new pair of shoes." Rule: Whenever we refer to a singular countable noun, it must be preceded by an article such as 'a' or 'an', depending on the sound of the next word. The correct sentence should be: "Alka bought a new pair of shoes for the party.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. He is / an expert / in / an English literature
questions.explanation: B)an English literature'में 'an' का use गलत है क्योंकि 'literature' एक uncountable noun है और इसके पहले कोई article ('a' या 'an') का use नहीं होता। सही वाक्य होना चाहिए: "He is an expert in English literature." The error lies in option B ('an English literature'). The article 'an' is incorrectly used because 'literature' is an uncountable noun and does not take any article before it. The correct phrase should be "in English literature."

questions.question: Identify the option that arranges the given parts in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. P) The Global Hunger Index (GHI) 2022 has shown India slipping further to the 107th position out of 121 countries from last year’s rank of 101 and being behind Pakistan, Bangladesh and Nepal. Q) But GHI claims that it uses the data presented by India to the UN to measure the proportion of undernourishment in children under five through the indicators of undernourishment, child wasting, child stunting and child mortality. R) Nobel laureate Abhijit Banerjee, too, was reported to have said that India should not take the drop in its ranking seriously as the methodology used for the exercise was ‘model-based’ and not survey-based. S) Last year, too, when its rank had fallen as compared to that in 2020, India had slammed the report, saying that the basis of calculating the findings — prepared by Irish aid agency Concern Worldwide and German organisation Welt Hunger Hilfe — was divorced from ground reality and that the methodology used was unscientific.
questions.explanation: B) PSRQ P:Sentence P sets up the problem by providing the current Global Hunger Index ranking for India. This is the main theme introduced.S:Sentence S comes next because it refers to "last year" and the past criticism from India about the methodology, which ties back to the ranking mentioned in Sentence P. The pronoun "its" in Sentence S refers to India mentioned in Sentence P, demonstrating the noun-pronoun relationshipR:Sentence R follows as it expands on the criticism of the methodology introduced in Sentence S. This sentence brings a new perspective, that of Nobel laureate Abhijit Banerjee. He suggests that the methodology used by the GHI is flawed, which corroborates with India's criticism in Sentence SQ:Sentence Q is last because it introduces the response from GHI to the criticism. The word "But" at the beginning of the sentence is a clear indicator that it's providing a contrasting point of view to the arguments mentioned in sentences R and S.

questions.question: COMPREHENSION In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. A recent article in Nature that studied the ______1______of Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease (MAFLD) among IT employees in Hyderabad had alarming findings: 84% of the participants had fatty liver, indicating MAFLD, and 71% were obese. This striking statistic underscores a larger, ______2_______insidious public health crisis that is emerging in tandem ______3_____ the growth of India’s urban economy. The underlying drivers are chronic stress, excess salt intake, disrupted sleep patterns, and prolonged sedentary routines, particularly _______4______ those in the technology sector. IT companies often _______5________employees to their desks by offering free kiosks stocked with snacks that fall short of nutritional standards. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: C) prevalencePrevalence' का use होगा क्योंकि "prevalence" का अर्थ होता है किसी बीमारी या स्थिति का व्यापक रूप से फैला होना। Passage में MAFLD (Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease) के फैलाव के बारे में बात की गई है, इसलिए 'prevalence' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Endurance' का अर्थ है सहनशीलता, 'intelligence' का अर्थ है बुद्धिमत्ता, और 'Resilience' का अर्थ है लचीलापन, जो इस context में सही नहीं है।'Prevalence' will be used because it means the widespread occurrence of a disease or condition. The passage discusses the spread of MAFLD (Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease) among IT employees, making 'prevalence' the correct choice. Whereas, 'Endurance' means stamina, 'intelligence' means intellect, and 'Resilience' means flexibility, which do not fit in this context.

questions.question: COMPREHENSION In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. A recent article in Nature that studied the ______1______of Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease (MAFLD) among IT employees in Hyderabad had alarming findings: 84% of the participants had fatty liver, indicating MAFLD, and 71% were obese. This striking statistic underscores a larger, ______2_______insidious public health crisis that is emerging in tandem ______3_____ the growth of India’s urban economy. The underlying drivers are chronic stress, excess salt intake, disrupted sleep patterns, and prolonged sedentary routines, particularly _______4______ those in the technology sector. IT companies often _______5________employees to their desks by offering free kiosks stocked with snacks that fall short of nutritional standards. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: A) more 'more' का use होगा क्योंकि यहाँ एक बड़ी और अधिक गंभीर (insidious) सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य संकट की तुलना की जा रही है। "more" का प्रयोग adjective (insidious) की degree बढ़ाने के लिए किया गया है, जो grammatically सही है। much: यह uncountable nouns के साथ प्रयोग होता है, लेकिन यहाँ "insidious" (adjective) को modify करना है। more than: यह comparative structure में दो चीज़ों की तुलना के लिए प्रयोग होता है, लेकिन यहाँ केवल "insidious" को emphasize करना है। than: यह comparative degree में दूसरे भाग के साथ आता है (जैसे "more dangerous than"), लेकिन यहाँ कोई तुलना नहीं हो रही। English Explanation:'more' will be used because it intensifies the adjective "insidious," indicating a larger and increasingly severe public health crisis. It is grammatically correct as "more" is used before adjectives to show a higher degree.much: Used with uncountable nouns, not to modify adjectives like "insidious." more than: Used for direct comparisons (e.g., "more than before"), but no comparison is made here. than: Requires a second element in comparison (e.g., "worse than"), which is absent here

questions.question: COMPREHENSION In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. A recent article in Nature that studied the ______1______of Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease (MAFLD) among IT employees in Hyderabad had alarming findings: 84% of the participants had fatty liver, indicating MAFLD, and 71% were obese. This striking statistic underscores a larger, ______2_______insidious public health crisis that is emerging in tandem ______3_____ the growth of India’s urban economy. The underlying drivers are chronic stress, excess salt intake, disrupted sleep patterns, and prolonged sedentary routines, particularly _______4______ those in the technology sector. IT companies often _______5________employees to their desks by offering free kiosks stocked with snacks that fall short of nutritional standards. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: B) With'With' का use होगा क्योंकि "with" का अर्थ होता है साथ में या के साथ। यहाँ sentence में 'emerging in tandem with' का मतलब है कि public health crisis भारत के urban economy के विकास के साथ-साथ बढ़ रही है। 'With' यहाँ सही है क्योंकि यह दो चीजों के समानांतर होने को दर्शाता है। जबकि 'by' का अर्थ है द्वारा, 'for' का अर्थ है के लिए, और 'to' का अर्थ है को, जो इस context में सही नहीं हैं।'With' will be used because it means "alongside" or "together with." Here, the phrase 'emerging in tandem with' indicates that the public health crisis is growing parallel to India's urban economy. 'With' is correct here as it shows the simultaneous occurrence of two things. Whereas, 'by' means "through the agency of," 'for' means "intended for," and 'to' means "toward," which are not appropriate in this context.

questions.question: COMPREHENSION In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. A recent article in Nature that studied the ______1______of Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease (MAFLD) among IT employees in Hyderabad had alarming findings: 84% of the participants had fatty liver, indicating MAFLD, and 71% were obese. This striking statistic underscores a larger, ______2_______insidious public health crisis that is emerging in tandem ______3_____ the growth of India’s urban economy. The underlying drivers are chronic stress, excess salt intake, disrupted sleep patterns, and prolonged sedentary routines, particularly _______4______ those in the technology sector. IT companies often _______5________employees to their desks by offering free kiosks stocked with snacks that fall short of nutritional standards. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: A) among'Among' का use होगा क्योंकि यह एक group (विशेषकर technology sector के लोगों) के बीच की स्थिति को दर्शाता है। यहाँ 'those in the technology sector' एक विशिष्ट group है, इसलिए 'among' सही है। 'Amongest' गलत है क्योंकि यह कोई valid word नहीं है। 'Amid' और 'amidst' का use general surroundings/atmosphere के लिए होता है, किसी specific group के लिए नहीं।'Among' will be used because it indicates being part of a group (especially people in the technology sector). Here, 'those in the technology sector' is a specific group, so 'among' is correct. 'Amongest' is incorrect as it is not a valid word. 'Amid' and 'amidst' are used for general surroundings/atmosphere, not for a specific group.

questions.question: COMPREHENSION In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. A recent article in Nature that studied the ______1______of Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Fatty Liver Disease (MAFLD) among IT employees in Hyderabad had alarming findings: 84% of the participants had fatty liver, indicating MAFLD, and 71% were obese. This striking statistic underscores a larger, ______2_______insidious public health crisis that is emerging in tandem ______3_____ the growth of India’s urban economy. The underlying drivers are chronic stress, excess salt intake, disrupted sleep patterns, and prolonged sedentary routines, particularly _______4______ those in the technology sector. IT companies often _______5________employees to their desks by offering free kiosks stocked with snacks that fall short of nutritional standards. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: D) Tether 'Tether' का use होगा क्योंकि "tether" का अर्थ होता है किसी को किसी स्थान पर बांधे रखना। यहाँ sentence में mention किया गया है कि IT कंपनियाँ अपने employees को उनकी डेस्क से बांधे रखती हैं snacks के माध्यम से, इसलिए 'tether' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'exodus' का अर्थ है बड़ी संख्या में लोगों का प्रस्थान, 'hackneyed' का अर्थ है घिसा-पिटा, और 'dislodging' का अर्थ है हटाना, जो इस context में सही नहीं है।'Tether' will be used because it means to tie or restrict someone to a place. The sentence mentions that IT companies keep employees tied to their desks by offering free snacks, making 'tether' the correct choice. Whereas, 'exodus' means a mass departure, 'hackneyed' means overused/clichéd, and 'dislodging' means removing, which do not fit in this context. 'Tether' is a transitive verb that correctly fits the structure of the sentence ("companies tether employees").
